headline: Bristol Myers Squibb expands Nvidia AI infrastructure for drug discovery
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) has expanded its artificial intelligence collaboration with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), announcing plans to deploy an Nvidia DGX SuperPOD powered by DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems to strengthen its AI infrastructure for drug discovery and development. The expansion builds on a partnership that began nearly three years ago and is designed to provide significantly greater computing power for AI-driven research.

The expansion builds on a partnership that began nearly three years ago and is designed to provide significantly greater computing power for AI-driven research.

headline: Merck’s Keytruda Faces a Patent Cliff. These New Cancer Drugs Could Take Over.
summary: For many cancers now, the standard treatment is chemotherapy plus an immunotherapy such as  Merck  Keytruda, the best-selling drug in the world.  Many think it will pair two innovations: precisely-targeted chemo drugs known as antibody-drug conjugates, or ADCs, and augmented immunotherapies in the form of bispecific antibodies.  Bispecific antibodies are being tested by  Bristol Myers Squibb  (with its partner  BioNTech  ),  AstraZeneca  and the biotech firm  Summit Therapeutics  ADCs have already rung up big sales for Astra,  Pfizer Gilead Sciences  and  Roche Holding  but Merck has a strong contender in development.

Bispecific antibodies are being tested by  Bristol Myers Squibb  (with its partner  BioNTech  ),  AstraZeneca  and the biotech firm  Summit Therapeutics  ADCs have already rung up big sales for Astra,  Pfizer Gilead Sciences  and  Roche Holding  but Merck has a strong contender in development.

headline: Bristol Myers buys Nvidia's latest AI computing system for drug research
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb said on Monday it is buying the latest-generation computing system from chip company Nvidia ‌to support its use of artificial intelligence across its drug discovery and development ‌operations.  The drugmaker said it will be the first life sciences company to buy an Nvidia DGX SuperPOD ​based on its Vera Rubin systems.  The chipmaker unveiled its Vera Rubin architecture earlier this year as the successor to its current generation of AI computing systems.
